Hello, as @Eryman pointed out, we already have an achievement "Gold Fever". So please don't translate this one as "Fever". In Czech, we use the word "Honba" which is more close to "Rush" and means something like "Hunt" or "Chase". Thanks.

To whomever downvoting/upvoting the other Ukrainian translation option: translating from the russian translation doesn't count as translation or localization, especially under current circumstances. Please provide your more creative, vivid, and appropriate options instead.

trans-unit.note:
It's quite hard to find an equivalent (but still catchy) word for "Evergreen" in portuguese so I went searching. In case it helps anyone:
https://www.urbandictionary.com/define.php?term=Evergreen%20State

Evergreen é um branding do estado portanto deve permanecer não traduzido
EN: Evergreen is an state branding, so it must remain untranslated

trans-unit.note: I propose not to translate it > https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Union_Pacific_Big_Boy
@SpanishProofreader I fully agree. Seems easier to just use it due to the achievement text.

I know I'm late to the party, but I'd say this could be translated to any other big or legendary local train if there's one, since Big Boy is not that well-known other than probably locals and connection with Cheyenne/Wyoming is also not recogniced. Most people would propably think that it's just not translated yet if left untranslated on purpose. Atleast us Finns don't usually use that much English phrases and it would make more sense to have it translated. The achievement text will still fit and would miss only title's slight connection with hauling something heavy.

context-group.context:
NEW_ACHIEVEMENT_98_1_NAME
Id: NEW_ACHIEVEMENT_98_1_NAME Achievement name for American Truck Simulator shown on the Steam website (http://steamcommunity.com/stats/270880/achievements/). Must fit within 44 characters. Use the same units as in the English version. Translation can be something corresponding to the idea of the original title in your language or culture. The titles usually have somewhat satirical tone.

trans-unit.note: "Maj" in Swedish means May, and "majs" means corn. This is the best I can do to honor the original "Cornfusion". We have a play on words with "maj" (the month) and "majs" (corn), respectively. The sentance reads "In the middle of [maj(s)]", thereby aknowledging the maze as well. Feel free to come up with a pun that still honors both the things in the original. :-)

trans-unit.source: Drive to the center of the Evergreen cornfield maze

trans-unit.source: Complete perfect deliveries (no damage, no fines, on time) between the following cities: Kennewick - Lewiston; Boise - Twin Falls; Twin Falls - Pocatello; Pocatello - Idaho Falls. Any order or direction counts (requires Washington and Idaho DLCs)
trans-unit.target: 명시된 도시를 오가는 화물을 완벽하게 (파손 없이, 벌금 없이, 정시에) 배송하십시오 : 케너윅-루이스턴, 보이시-트윈폴스, 트윈폴스-포커텔로, 포커텔로-아이다호폴스. 주행 방향, 운송 순서에 관계없이 카운트됩니다 (Washington과 Idaho DLC가 필요함)
